title: chrysopsis aspera
intro: Chrysopsis aspera, also known as the rough sea-blush or rough bay-aster, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Chrysopsis aspera are attributed to its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Chrysopsis aspera can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
